global_context.scene_description: A photorealistic close-up of a fake horror-movie-style poster taped to the side of a kitchen refrigerator. The poster has dramatic horror-genre design: dark background with deep red typography, ominous lighting, the title in massive bold blood-red letters reads '$15,000 AC REPLACEMENT'. Below the title, in smaller italicized creepy font: 'A NIGHTMARE EVERY HOMEOWNER FEARS'. There's a stylized graphic of an AC unit looming menacingly. The poster is pinned with magnets at the corners. ACROSS THE POSTER, a bright cheerful kelly-green sticker is stuck diagonally — the sticker reads in bold white type '0% APR. APPROVED.' with a green checkmark. The sticker visually 'cancels' the horror poster — it's an obvious correction laid over a marketing exaggeration. The kitchen background is partially visible — a slice of stainless steel fridge, a casual home setting. Soft natural light. Mood: genre flip — the dread is fictional, the fix is real. Photorealistic, shot on 50mm lens.
